title: A process for the immunological detection of filarial infection
abstract: The present disclosure provides an improved immunological process for determining the presence of filarial third stage larval (L3) specific infection caused by a nematode parasite, Wuchereria bancrofti. The process comprises contacting the infected sample with a recombinant antigen of SEQ ID No. 1 that specifically binds to the infective L3 stage specific antibodies (IgG1 and IgG4). Thus, the present disclosure provides a rapid and sensitive method for assessing the transmission of filarial infection at an early stage and also the past infection.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method for determining the filarial third stage larval (L-3) specific infection comprising the steps of: (i) providing a biological sample from a subject suspected of being exposed to Wuchereria bancrofti; (ii) contacting the diluted sample of step (i) with an immobilized recombinant antigen Wuchereria bancrofti Macrophage Migration Inhibition Factor (WhMIF-2) and incubating at 30-40Â°C for 1-4 hours to form an immunocomplex; (iii) adding diluted anti-human IgG HRP conjugated antibodies to the immunocomplex formed in step (ii) and allowed to incubate at 30-45Â°C for 1-4 hours to form immunoconj ugate ; (iv) reacting immunoconjugate of step (iii) with a detection reagent and allowed to incubate in dark for 10-30 minutes; (v) adding 2-6 N H 2 SO 4 and measuring the optical density. 2. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the recombinant antigen Wuchereria bancrofti Macrophage Migration Inhibition Factor (WhMIF-2) in step (ii) is encoded by a polypeptide of SEQ ID No.l. 3. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the anti-human IgG antibodies in step (iii) is selected from the group consisting of IgGl and IgG4 antibodies. 4. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the optical density at 462 nm for filarial third stage larva (L3) infected samples is in the range of 1.0 to 2.5. 5. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the biological sample is a human blood, serum, plasma sample. 6. A diagnostic kit for detecting the filarial third stage larval (L3) specific infection comprising: (i) purified recombinant antigen Wuchereria bancrofti Macrophage Migration Inhibition Factor ( ?MIF-2) of SEQ ID No. 1; 

 (ii) a buffer reagent: Phosphate Buffer Saline (PBS), Tween-20, Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA); (iii) detection reagent; and (iv) anti-human IgGland IgG4 antibody HRP conjugates. 7. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the method detects early stage lymphatic filariasis before 6 months.
